LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF IVAN RESENIKOFF'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES, COSTS AND EXPENSES. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED IN LARGE PART. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND, EXCEPT FOR THE REQUEST FOR A MULTIPLIER, GOOD CAUSE HAS BEEN SHOWN FOR ALL FEES, COSTS AND EXPENSES REQUESTED. PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED FEES OF $17,139.50 AND COSTS AND EXPENSES OF $5,141.85 FOR A TOTAL OF $22,281.35. THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION TO DENY A MULTIPLIER BECAUSE THE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE CIRCUMSTANCES IN THIS CASE WARRANT INCREASING THE LODESTAR AMOUNT. DEFENDANT FCA WITHDREW ITS MOTION TO TAX COSTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys' Fees, Costs And Expenses
Off Calendar Dec-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF IVAN RESENIKOFF'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES, COSTS AND EXPENSES

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Ivan Resnikoff's motion for attorneys' fees, costs and expenses is granted in large part. No opposition filed and, except for the request for a multiplier, good cause has been shown for all fees, costs and expenses requested. Plaintiff is awarded fees of $17,139.50 and costs and expenses of $5,141.85 for a total of $22,281.35. The court exercises its discretion to deny a multiplier because the plaintiff has not shown that the circumstances in this case warrant increasing the lodestar amount. Defendant FCA withdrew its motion to tax costs. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-28-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-07-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Discovery Responses Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 2031.320(A); Memorandum In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar May-19-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production From Defendant Fca Us Llc, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF IVAN RESENIKOFF'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production From Defendant Fca Us Llc, And Request For Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Ivan Resnikoff's motion to compel further responses to Request for Production Nos. 45 and 46 is denied. Resnikoff sought an order from this court that defendant FCA US LLC must produce documents evidencing complaints by 2023 Jeep Grand Cherokee owners that were similar to complaints made by Resnikoff (RFP 45) and documents evidencing any warranty repairs to any 2023 Jeep Grand Cherokee vehicles (RFP 46). FCA initially objected but after this motion was filed supplemented its answers and represented that it would produce responsive documents (Smith Dec. Ex. 12.) Ordinarily, a responding party's amendment while a discovery motion is pending does not moot the motion. (See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 390.) Here, however, Resnikoff's RFP 45 was vague (what complaints and how to discern similarity, where the complaint does not say what complaints Resnikoff had about the vehicle?), and his RFP 46 was very broad, and the parties have made more progress through their meet and confer efforts than the court could or would have ordered. Moreover, the reply brief does not indicate what further production if any Resnikoff seeks. Accordingly, in these circumstances no court order is appropriate. The court finds that both parties acted with substantial justification and does not award sanctions. An order is submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
